items.title: Concerns smelter closure exposes Australia to geopolitical risk

children.content: Australia's only manganese alloy smelter has closed, leaving the future of the site uncertain and the country more reliant on imported alloys for steel production.

articles.title: Liberty Bell Bay manganese smelter to close immediately

children.content: Australia's only manganese smelter, in northern Tasmania, will close immediately after administrators failed to find a buyer for the former GFG Alliance asset.

image.altText: smelter factory external shots

children.content: Australia's only manganese smelter is ceasing operations, leaving more than 200 employees without work in northern Tasmania.

image.altText: A man wearing a bright orange shirt standing outside

gallery.altText: A manganese smelter no longer in use, photographed from behind a fence.

thumbnail.altText: A manganese smelter no longer in use, photographed from behind a fence.

items.title: 'Pure evil': 76yo victim speaks after home invasion

children.content: The 76-year-old victim of a violent robbery on Tasmania's east coast has described the people who attacked him as "pure evil".

image.altText: Man smiles at camera with cows in back ground

items.title: Group including sawmillers calls for end to Tasmania's old-growth logging

children.content: A coalition of conservationists, artists and forest industry figures wants an end to old-growth logging in Tasmania but major environmental groups have distanced themselves from the "shared vision statement", saying it does not go far enough.

image.altText: Birds eye view of a forest region that's been logged and burned

items.title: Federal MP and staffer injured in 'man overboard' incident in Tasmania

children.content: Tasmanian police say a man fell from an oyster barge when it hit a sandbar and was briefly trapped. Member for Barker Tony Pasin says he and a staffer were injured and now receiving medical treatment, but it is not clear who fell from the barge.

image.altText: Exteriors of a public hospital.

items.title: Electoral boundary shakeup leaves politicians with a big decision

children.content: Some notable politicians are among the 114,000 Tasmanians displaced by electoral boundary changes, and now have a difficult decision to make — stick with the electorate that voted them in, or move with the communities they've long been aligned with and live in.

image.altText: Hon Rebecca White MP

items.title: Transgender woman's 'humiliating' experience with major corporations

children.content: What should have been a quick process of updating personal details left Laura Mitchell "insulted" and "humiliated" by the country's largest institutions.

gallery.altText: Woman with brown hair looks out at the water while standing at Frankston Pier.

thumbnail.altText: Woman with brown hair looks out at the water while standing at Frankston Pier.

items.title: Sam Neill's cause of death revealed by agent

children.content: New Zealand actor Sam Neill died from pneumonia, his long-time agent Philip Grenz has confirmed.

image.altText: Sam Neill wears a scarf around his neck and jacket. Behind are black and white images of him over the years

items.title: ANU head apologises for failing to protect Jewish staff, students

children.content: The interim vice-chancellor of the Australian National University has apologised for the institution's failure to protect the psychological safety of Jewish students and staff during pro-Palestinian encampments in 2024.

image.altText: A woman with long blonde hair wearing a black blazer stands in a conference room looking serious.

items.title: Drug traffickers jailed over failed plot to smuggle cocaine from PNG by plane

children.content: Two masterminds behind a 2020 plot to bring $80 million worth of cocaine into Australia were sentenced in Victoria’s County Court, five years after a failed drug trafficking flight.

items.title: Moira Deeming to be disendorsed as a Liberal candidate after dropping court action

children.content: Victoria's Liberal Party is set to drop Moira Deeming as an upper house candidate following contested claims against former state leader Matthew Guy.

image.altText: A woman leaving a meeting room in parliament

items.title: Geelong win over Saints soured by injury to star Holmes

children.content: Geelong beats St Kilda at Kardinia Park to kick off round 19 of the AFL, but the injury list gets longer with a concerning leg issue for Max Holmes.

items.title: Why Australians shouldn't panic about fuel prices as war in Iran escalates again

children.content: With the price of oil rising and the fuel excise cut due to end, drivers are facing twin threats when it comes to the price of fuel, but experts say Australia is in a much better position compared with when the Middle East conflict began.

articles.title: Iran frees US citizen after almost two years of imprisonment

children.content: Iran has freed a US-Iranian citizen detained since December, 2024 in what Trump says was a 'gesture of goodwill'.

items.title: 'Walsh was unbelievable': Broncos keep season alive with upset win

children.content: Brisbane fullback Reece Walsh has been described as "unbelievable" by Penrith's coach after leading the Broncos to an upset win on Thursday, ending an eight-match losing streak and keeping their slim finals chances alive.

children.content: Halfway through the Race Around The World competition, I'm second last on the leaderboard. So, I decide to cheat.

items.title: Too many cooks spoil The Odyssey's bubbling epic

children.content: The scope of Christopher Nolan's latest epic might be awesome but a menagerie of supporting faces and questionable characterisations muddle the director's vision.

items.title: BHP staff down tools for historic strike in Western Australia

items.title: You'll now be able to spot an AI-generated song on Spotify

children.content: Streaming giant Spotify has stopped short of expunging artificially generated music from its platform, but it is making it easier for listeners to work out that they are listening to tracks produced using AI.

articles.title: Community meets over plans for large 'AI factory' at River Murray town

children.content: An AI company says its proposed projects have the potential to create hundreds of jobs in South Australia, but residents of one River Murray town say they want to know more about the potential impacts.

image.altText: Tailem Bend locals at a public forum.

articles.title: Albanese rolls out rule book and red carpet for AI companies

children.content: If Australia is truly going to coax major AI companies to choose us rather than the other suitor nations with similar ambitions, some tough decisions need to be made soon.

items.title: Q-Day is coming and it might break the entire internet

children.content: The countdown is on to Q-Day, when an algorithm written more than 30 years ago could run on a quantum computer and compromise the security of data on the internet. How are we preparing?

items.title: Death-defying Argentina living every World Cup game as if it's the last

children.content: Argentina has been the daredevils of this World Cup, and after another incredible comeback to defeat England in the semifinals, its heady mix of emotion and skill, might just deliver back-to-back titles.
